name: GitHub branch protection (built-in)
label: GitHub branch protection (built-in)
changesWhen: Compliance requires stricter review count or signed commits
whenToUse: Every repo deploying to production
whenNot: Never skip for prod repos
primaryTradeoff: vs GitLab protected branches: GitHub has fine-grained token control; GitLab has more granular merge approval counts

name: GitHub CODEOWNERS (built-in)
label: GitHub CODEOWNERS (built-in)
changesWhen: Team ownership changes; new service paths added
whenToUse: Always — auto-assigns reviewers on paths containing secrets config, IaC, or auth code
whenNot: Never skip for security-critical paths
primaryTradeoff: vs manual reviewer assignment: CODEOWNERS auto-assigns; manual assignment requires each PR author to know the owner

name: GitHub Actions OIDC (built-in)
label: GitHub Actions OIDC (built-in)
changesWhen: Cloud provider migration; IAM role ARN or trust policy changes
ciIntegration: GitHub native — permissions: id-token: write in workflow
whenToUse: Always — eliminates long-lived static credentials from CI
whenNot: Use stored secrets only when the cloud provider does not support OIDC federation
primaryTradeoff: vs static secrets: OIDC tokens expire per job; static secrets persist until manually rotated

name: GHCR — GitHub Container Registry
label: GHCR — GitHub Container Registry
changesWhen: Org transfer; visibility policy changes
whenToUse: Default for GitHub-hosted repos — free private registry, tight OIDC integration
whenNot: Use ECR when AWS VPC endpoint access is required by compliance
primaryTradeoff: vs ECR: GHCR needs no AWS account; ECR has deeper IAM and VPC endpoint policy control

name: ECR — Elastic Container Registry
label: ECR — Elastic Container Registry
license: AWS — pay per GB stored + transferred
changesWhen: AWS account migration; ECR lifecycle policy changes
whenToUse: AWS workloads requiring VPC endpoint registry access or cross-account pull policies
whenNot: Skip when not on AWS
primaryTradeoff: vs GHCR: ECR has deeper AWS IAM integration; GHCR needs no AWS account

name: Kyverno v1.13
label: Kyverno v1.13
changesWhen: New image registries added; policy rules updated; new namespaces scoped
whenToUse: Any Kubernetes cluster — blocks unsigned images at admission before the pod starts
whenNot: Skip for non-Kubernetes deployments (serverless, VMs)
primaryTradeoff: vs OPA/Gatekeeper: Kyverno policies are plain Kubernetes YAML; Gatekeeper requires Rego language

name: Renovate v43
label: Renovate v43
changesWhen: Automerge rules change; grouping rules updated; new registries added
ciIntegration: GitHub Action or self-hosted Renovate bot
whenToUse: Multiple repos or monorepos needing grouped, automerge-capable dependency updates
whenNot: Use Dependabot when zero-config GitHub-native updates are sufficient
primaryTradeoff: vs Dependabot: Renovate has grouping, automerge, and multi-registry support; Dependabot needs no config file

name: Dependabot (built-in)
label: Dependabot (built-in)
changesWhen: Ignore rules change; schedule changes
whenToUse: Simple single-ecosystem repos where Renovate config overhead is not justified
whenNot: Replace with Renovate for monorepos or multi-ecosystem repos
primaryTradeoff: vs Renovate: Dependabot needs zero setup; Renovate needs renovate.json but gives far more control

name: SonarLint (VS Code / JetBrains)
label: SonarLint (VS Code / JetBrains)
changesWhen: Connected mode: SonarQube/Cloud ruleset updates
whenToUse: Any developer wanting real-time SAST feedback without waiting for CI
whenNot: Skip for editors without SonarLint support
primaryTradeoff: vs CodeQL IDE extension: SonarLint works in VS Code and JetBrains; CodeQL IDE is VS Code only

name: hadolint IDE extension
label: hadolint IDE extension
changesWhen: hadolint ruleset updates
whenToUse: Any developer writing Dockerfiles — catches FROM, RUN, and layer issues inline
whenNot: Skip for non-container workloads
primaryTradeoff: vs manual Dockerfile review: hadolint checks all instructions automatically; manual review misses subtle layer ordering issues

name: pre-commit v3.8 (framework)
label: pre-commit v3.8 (framework)
changesWhen: Hooks added or removed; .pre-commit-config.yaml changes
whenToUse: Every repo — manages all hooks in one config file, runs on git commit
whenNot: Never skip on repos with a CI gate
primaryTradeoff: vs husky: pre-commit supports all languages; husky is Node.js-only

name: gitleaks v8.21
label: gitleaks v8.21
changesWhen: Custom regex patterns added for org-specific secret formats
whenToUse: Every repo — blocks commit if a secret pattern is detected in staged files
whenNot: Never skip
primaryTradeoff: vs truffleHog: gitleaks is faster with deterministic regex rules; truffleHog adds entropy-based detection for unknown formats

name: hadolint v2.12
label: hadolint v2.12
changesWhen: Best practice rule updates
whenToUse: Any repo containing Dockerfiles
whenNot: Skip for repos with no Dockerfiles
primaryTradeoff: vs dockerfile-lint: hadolint is actively maintained with more rules; dockerfile-lint is unmaintained

name: checkov v3
label: checkov v3
changesWhen: New IaC resource types added; policy framework updates
ciIntegration: Run as pre-commit hook via bridgecrewio/checkov
whenToUse: Any repo with IaC files
whenNot: Skip for repos with no IaC
primaryTradeoff: vs KICS: checkov has broader multi-framework support; KICS has deeper Terraform-specific rule coverage

name: gitleaks v8.21
label: gitleaks v8.21
changesWhen: Custom rules added; .gitleaks.toml baseline updated
whenToUse: Every repo — runs on staged files at git commit, blocks before push
whenNot: Never skip
primaryTradeoff: vs truffleHog: gitleaks is faster for pre-commit use; truffleHog v3 scans full repo history with entropy detection

name: pre-commit v3.8
label: pre-commit v3.8
changesWhen: Hook config changes; new hooks added to .pre-commit-config.yaml
whenToUse: Every PR — re-runs hooks in CI to catch bypassed local hooks (--no-verify)
whenNot: Never skip
primaryTradeoff: vs running hooks individually: pre-commit manages all hooks in one config; individual runs require separate CI steps

name: Trivy v0.58 (fs mode)
label: Trivy v0.58 (fs mode)
appliesTo: All — npm, pip, Maven, Go, Cargo, RubyGems, NuGet
changesWhen: New package ecosystems added; CVE severity thresholds change
whenToUse: Default — one tool scans all package managers without extra config
whenNot: Use Snyk when commercial fix PR automation or SBOM export is required
primaryTradeoff: vs Snyk: Trivy is free and open source; Snyk auto-creates fix PRs and has better remediation UX

name: Snyk v1.1280
label: Snyk v1.1280
changesWhen: Snyk org policies change; ignored CVEs list changes
whenToUse: When the team needs Snyk's automated fix PR and license scanning in one platform
whenNot: Replace with Trivy when cost is a constraint
primaryTradeoff: vs Trivy: Snyk auto-creates fix PRs; Trivy only reports findings — no auto-remediation

name: OWASP Dependency-Check v10.0
label: OWASP Dependency-Check v10.0
changesWhen: NVD API key rotation; NVD data feed schema changes
whenToUse: Java/Maven/Gradle repos where NVD-sourced CVE data is required by compliance policy
whenNot: Skip when Trivy covers the language ecosystem and NVD-specific sourcing is not required
primaryTradeoff: vs Trivy: Dependency-Check queries NVD directly; Trivy aggregates multiple advisory databases

name: osv-scanner v1.8
label: osv-scanner v1.8
appliesTo: All — lockfile-based: npm, pip, Maven, Go, Cargo, RubyGems, NuGet, Composer, Gradle
changesWhen: OSV database updated continuously; new lockfile formats added
whenToUse: When Google OSV database coverage is required — broader advisory source than NVD alone; lockfile-based with zero false positives from missing lockfiles
whenNot: Skip when Trivy fs mode is already configured and OSV-specific sourcing is not required by policy
primaryTradeoff: vs Trivy: osv-scanner uses the OSV database (superset of NVD for many ecosystems); Trivy aggregates multiple sources including OSV

name: CodeQL v2.19
label: CodeQL v2.19
appliesTo: JS/TS, Python, Java, Go, C/C++, Ruby, Swift
changesWhen: Language support matrix expands; query packs updated
whenToUse: Default for GitHub repos on supported languages — deep dataflow and taint tracking
whenNot: Use Semgrep for unsupported languages or rapid custom rule authoring
primaryTradeoff: vs Semgrep: CodeQL does deep semantic dataflow analysis; Semgrep has simpler rule syntax and faster scan time

name: Semgrep v1.75
label: Semgrep v1.75
changesWhen: Custom ruleset updated; new language added to repo
whenToUse: Languages not supported by CodeQL, or when custom security rules are needed in days not weeks
whenNot: Skip when CodeQL coverage is sufficient for the languages in use
primaryTradeoff: vs CodeQL: Semgrep rules are easier to write; CodeQL has deeper interprocedural analysis

name: SonarCloud / SonarQube v10.5
label: SonarCloud / SonarQube v10.5
changesWhen: Quality gate rules updated; language analysis plugins updated
output: SonarCloud PR decoration + JSON via API
whenToUse: When code quality metrics (duplication, complexity, maintainability ratings) are needed alongside security findings
whenNot: Skip when CodeQL coverage is sufficient and code quality gates are tracked elsewhere
primaryTradeoff: vs CodeQL: SonarCloud covers code quality and security in one tool; CodeQL does deeper security dataflow analysis only

name: FOSSA v3
label: FOSSA v3
changesWhen: Allowed license policy updated; new ecosystems added
whenToUse: Commercial products where GPL or AGPL in a dependency creates legal risk — FOSSA blocks PRs automatically
whenNot: Use ORT or licensefinder for internal tools with no strict compliance requirement
primaryTradeoff: vs ORT: FOSSA enforces policy automatically and has commercial support; ORT is open source and self-hostable

name: ORT — OSS Review Toolkit v25.0
label: ORT — OSS Review Toolkit v25.0
appliesTo: All — npm, Maven, pip, Cargo, Go modules, NuGet, RubyGems
changesWhen: Policy rules updated; new package managers added to monorepo
whenToUse: Monorepos with multiple package managers needing one open-source license audit tool
whenNot: Use FOSSA when automated PR-blocking policy enforcement is required
primaryTradeoff: vs FOSSA: ORT is fully open source and self-hostable; FOSSA has automated enforcement and commercial SLA

name: licensefinder v7
label: licensefinder v7
changesWhen: Approved licenses list changes in doc/dependency_decisions.yml
whenToUse: Internal tools needing a simple license audit without commercial tooling or ORT complexity
whenNot: Replace with FOSSA or ORT when automated policy enforcement is needed
primaryTradeoff: vs FOSSA: licensefinder is free and simple; FOSSA has automated policy enforcement and commercial support

name: checkov v3
label: checkov v3
appliesTo: Terraform / Helm / K8s / CloudFormation / Dockerfile / ARM / Ansible
changesWhen: New resource types added; policy framework updated
whenToUse: Any repo with IaC files — broadest multi-framework coverage in one tool
whenNot: Skip for repos with no IaC. Use KICS for deeper Terraform-specific rules.
primaryTradeoff: vs KICS: checkov covers more frameworks; KICS has stronger Terraform rule depth

name: KICS v2.1
label: KICS v2.1
changesWhen: Policy rules updated; new resource types added
whenToUse: Repos where Terraform is the primary IaC and maximum Terraform rule coverage is needed
whenNot: Skip when checkov is already configured
primaryTradeoff: vs checkov: KICS has deeper Terraform rules; checkov has broader multi-framework support

name: Trivy v0.58 (config mode) — replaces tfsec
label: Trivy v0.58 (config mode) — replaces tfsec
changesWhen: Same as Trivy fs/image mode — single Trivy version manages all scan modes
whenToUse: When Trivy is already used for SCA or container scanning — reuse same tool for IaC with no extra install
whenNot: Skip when checkov or KICS is already configured. Note: tfsec was absorbed into this mode in 2023.
primaryTradeoff: vs checkov: Trivy config consolidates all Trivy scans in one binary; checkov has broader ARM and Ansible coverage

name: Snyk IaC (part of Snyk CLI v1.1280+)
label: Snyk IaC (part of Snyk CLI v1.1280+)
tool: Snyk IaC (part of Snyk CLI v1.1280+)
changesWhen: Snyk org IaC policies change
whenToUse: When Snyk SCA is already deployed and consolidating to one vendor reduces tool sprawl
whenNot: Skip when checkov is already configured — duplicate IaC scanning adds noise
primaryTradeoff: vs checkov: Snyk IaC consolidates with Snyk SCA in one platform; checkov is open source with no commercial dependency

name: gitleaks v8.21
label: gitleaks v8.21
changesWhen: Custom regex patterns added for org-specific secret formats
whenToUse: Default — scans full repo history and PR diff for known secret patterns
whenNot: Never skip
primaryTradeoff: vs truffleHog: gitleaks is faster with deterministic rules; truffleHog adds entropy detection for non-patterned secrets

name: truffleHog v3.80
label: truffleHog v3.80
changesWhen: Entropy threshold tuned; custom detectors added
whenToUse: When entropy-based detection is needed for non-patterned secrets that gitleaks misses
whenNot: Skip when gitleaks rule coverage is sufficient
primaryTradeoff: vs gitleaks: truffleHog finds secrets without fixed patterns; gitleaks is faster on known patterns

name: GitHub Advanced Security (GHAS) — Secret Scanning
label: GitHub Advanced Security (GHAS) — Secret Scanning
license: Included with GitHub Advanced Security — free for public repos; paid for private repos on GitHub Enterprise
changesWhen: Custom patterns added via GHAS configuration; new partner secret types added by GitHub automatically
ciIntegration: GitHub native — enabled in Settings → Security → Secret scanning
whenToUse: GitHub Enterprise repos with GHAS license — scans every push and full historical commits with zero config
whenNot: Use gitleaks when GHAS is not available (GitHub Free/Team plans without GHAS add-on)
primaryTradeoff: vs gitleaks: GHAS requires no config file and covers all history automatically; gitleaks requires .gitleaks.toml but works on any plan

name: Docker buildx v0.15
label: Docker buildx v0.15
changesWhen: Multi-platform targets change; BuildKit feature flags change
whenToUse: Default for GitHub-hosted runners with Docker daemon access
whenNot: Use kaniko when Docker daemon is unavailable in the CI runner
primaryTradeoff: vs kaniko: Docker buildx requires Docker daemon; kaniko runs rootless inside Kubernetes pods

name: kaniko v1.23
label: kaniko v1.23
changesWhen: Base image changes; Kubernetes executor config changes
whenToUse: Kubernetes-native CI runners where Docker daemon is unavailable (Tekton, Argo Workflows)
whenNot: Skip when Docker buildx is sufficient
primaryTradeoff: vs Docker buildx: kaniko runs without daemon; Docker buildx needs daemon access

name: Buildah v1.36
label: Buildah v1.36
changesWhen: Buildah policy.json changes; storage driver changes
whenToUse: RHEL or OpenShift environments where Red Hat tooling is standardized; rootless builds without Docker or Kubernetes
whenNot: Skip when Docker buildx or kaniko is already configured
primaryTradeoff: vs kaniko: Buildah is OCI-native and RHEL-standard; kaniko is designed specifically for Kubernetes CI pods

name: Syft v1.6
label: Syft v1.6
appliesTo: All — container images + filesystems, all major package managers
changesWhen: New package ecosystems added; SBOM format changes
whenToUse: Default — generates SBOM from the built image tarball before container scan
whenNot: Use cdxgen for Java/Maven/Gradle repos needing class-level dependency resolution
primaryTradeoff: vs cdxgen: Syft is faster and multi-ecosystem; cdxgen has deeper JVM class-level resolution

name: cdxgen v10.1
label: cdxgen v10.1
changesWhen: New Java frameworks added; language analysis plugins updated
whenToUse: Java/Maven/Gradle repos where Syft misses class-level transitive dependencies
whenNot: Use Syft when multi-ecosystem scanning is needed — cdxgen has deeper JVM but narrower overall ecosystem support
primaryTradeoff: vs Syft: cdxgen resolves JVM class-level dependencies; Syft is faster and covers more package managers

name: Trivy v0.58 (image mode)
label: Trivy v0.58 (image mode)
changesWhen: CVE database auto-updates; severity threshold changes
whenToUse: Default — scans OS packages and app dependencies without a pre-built SBOM
whenNot: Use Grype when Syft SBOM already exists and re-scanning from SBOM is preferred
primaryTradeoff: vs Grype: Trivy scans without a pre-generated SBOM; Grype uses a Syft SBOM for faster re-scanning

name: Grype v0.82
label: Grype v0.82
changesWhen: Grype DB auto-updates; Syft SBOM schema version changes
whenToUse: When Syft SBOM already exists in the pipeline and re-scanning from SBOM is faster than a full image pull
whenNot: Skip when Trivy is already configured and Syft SBOM is not pre-generated
primaryTradeoff: vs Trivy: Grype re-scans from a Syft SBOM (faster on repeat runs); Trivy scans the image directly every time

name: Docker Scout (Docker CLI v25+)
label: Docker Scout (Docker CLI v25+)
license: Free for GitHub Actions — public and private repos
changesWhen: Docker Scout policy rules updated; base image recommendations change
output: SARIF / JSON + GitHub PR comment with base image upgrade path
whenToUse: Docker Desktop or Docker Hub users — free scanning with actionable base image upgrade recommendations
whenNot: Skip when Trivy or Grype is already configured — duplicate scanning adds no extra coverage
primaryTradeoff: vs Trivy: Docker Scout recommends specific base image upgrades that fix CVEs; Trivy reports CVEs without upgrade paths

name: GitHub required reviewers + CODEOWNERS
label: GitHub required reviewers + CODEOWNERS
changesWhen: Team ownership changes; minimum reviewer count changes
output: N/A — merge is blocked until approved
whenToUse: Every PR on a repo that deploys to production
whenNot: Never skip for prod-adjacent repos
primaryTradeoff: vs auto-merge on green CI: human review catches logic and design issues that automated tools miss

name: pre-commit v3.8
label: pre-commit v3.8
changesWhen: Same as Phase 2. Trigger event changes from pull_request to push to main in workflow config.
ciIntegration: pre-commit/action@v3 — triggered on push to main
whenToUse: Every merge to main — catches any hooks bypassed with --no-verify before the merge was reviewed
whenNot: Never skip
primaryTradeoff: vs running hooks individually: pre-commit manages all hooks in one config; individual runs require separate CI steps

name: Trivy v0.58 (fs mode)
label: Trivy v0.58 (fs mode)
changesWhen: Same thresholds as Phase 2. SARIF upload targets main branch Security tab — not the PR branch.
ciIntegration: aquasecurity/trivy-action — triggered on push to main
whenToUse: Re-run on merged code — the merge commit is new code that was not scanned as a unit before merge
whenNot: Never skip
primaryTradeoff: vs Snyk: same as Phase 2

name: Snyk v1.1280
label: Snyk v1.1280
changesWhen: Same as Phase 2. Findings go to Snyk dashboard under the main branch project.
ciIntegration: snyk/actions — triggered on push to main
whenToUse: When Snyk SCA is already in use in Phase 2 — re-run on merged state to update main branch baseline
whenNot: Skip if Trivy covers the requirement
primaryTradeoff: vs Trivy: same as Phase 2

name: OWASP Dependency-Check v10.0
label: OWASP Dependency-Check v10.0
changesWhen: Same as Phase 2. NVD data feed results for the merged code.
whenToUse: Same as Phase 2 — re-run on merged code for NVD-sourced compliance reporting
whenNot: Skip when Trivy covers the requirement
primaryTradeoff: vs Trivy: same as Phase 2

name: osv-scanner v1.8
label: osv-scanner v1.8
appliesTo: All — lockfile-based: npm, pip, Maven, Go, Cargo, RubyGems, NuGet, Composer, Gradle
changesWhen: OSV database updated continuously; new lockfile formats added
whenToUse: When Google OSV database coverage is required — broader advisory source than NVD alone; lockfile-based with zero false positives from missing lockfiles
whenNot: Skip when Trivy fs mode is already configured and OSV-specific sourcing is not required by policy
primaryTradeoff: vs Trivy: osv-scanner uses the OSV database (superset of NVD for many ecosystems); Trivy aggregates multiple sources including OSV

name: CodeQL v2.19
label: CodeQL v2.19
appliesTo: JS/TS, Python, Java, Go, C/C++, Ruby, Swift
changesWhen: Same as Phase 2. Findings uploaded under the main branch — these become the baseline for future PR comparisons.
output: SARIF → GitHub Security tab (main branch baseline)
ciIntegration: github/codeql-action — triggered on push to main
whenToUse: Establish the SAST baseline for main. All future Phase 2 PR scans compare against this baseline.
whenNot: Never skip
primaryTradeoff: vs Semgrep: same as Phase 2

name: Semgrep v1.75
label: Semgrep v1.75
changesWhen: Same as Phase 2. Findings update main branch baseline in Semgrep dashboard.
whenToUse: Same as Phase 2 — re-run on merged state to update Semgrep main branch baseline
whenNot: Skip if CodeQL covers the requirement
primaryTradeoff: vs CodeQL: same as Phase 2

name: SonarCloud / SonarQube v10.5
label: SonarCloud / SonarQube v10.5
changesWhen: Same as Phase 2. Quality gate run against the merged state — updates SonarCloud project status.
whenToUse: Same as Phase 2 — re-run on merged state to keep SonarCloud project status current
whenNot: Skip if CodeQL and other SAST tools cover the requirement
primaryTradeoff: vs CodeQL: same as Phase 2

name: FOSSA v3
label: FOSSA v3
changesWhen: Same as Phase 2. Re-confirms merged state passes the same policy.
ciIntegration: fossa-contrib/fossa-action — triggered on push to main
whenToUse: Re-run on merged code — confirm the merge did not introduce a policy-violating dependency from either branch
whenNot: Never skip
primaryTradeoff: vs ORT: same as Phase 2

name: ORT v25.0
label: ORT v25.0
changesWhen: Same as Phase 2. Re-runs on merged state.
whenToUse: Same as Phase 2 — re-run on merged monorepo state
whenNot: Skip if FOSSA covers the requirement
primaryTradeoff: vs FOSSA: same as Phase 2

name: licensefinder v7
label: licensefinder v7
changesWhen: Same as Phase 2.
whenToUse: Same as Phase 2 — re-run on merged state
whenNot: Skip if FOSSA or ORT covers the requirement
primaryTradeoff: vs FOSSA: same as Phase 2

name: checkov v3
label: checkov v3
appliesTo: Terraform / Helm / K8s / CloudFormation / Dockerfile / ARM
changesWhen: Same as Phase 2. Re-runs on merged IaC files.
ciIntegration: bridgecrewio/checkov-action — triggered on push to main
whenToUse: Re-run on merged IaC state — catch misconfigurations introduced in either branch during the merge
whenNot: Skip for repos with no IaC
primaryTradeoff: vs KICS: same as Phase 2

name: KICS v2.1
label: KICS v2.1
changesWhen: Same as Phase 2. Re-runs on merged IaC.
whenToUse: Same as Phase 2 — re-run on merged Terraform state
whenNot: Skip when checkov is sufficient
primaryTradeoff: vs checkov: same as Phase 2

name: Trivy v0.58 (config mode)
label: Trivy v0.58 (config mode)
changesWhen: Same as Phase 2.
whenToUse: Same as Phase 2 — reuse existing Trivy installation for IaC scan
whenNot: Skip when checkov is already configured
primaryTradeoff: vs checkov: same as Phase 2

name: Snyk IaC
label: Snyk IaC
changesWhen: Same as Phase 2.
whenToUse: Same as Phase 2 — re-run on merged state for Snyk platform users
whenNot: Skip when checkov is sufficient
primaryTradeoff: vs checkov: same as Phase 2

name: gitleaks v8.21
label: gitleaks v8.21
changesWhen: Same patterns as Phase 2. Phase 3-specific: gitleaks baseline file path uses main branch context, not PR branch context.
ciIntegration: gitleaks/gitleaks-action — triggered on push to main
whenToUse: Scan the merge commit specifically — a secret that exists in neither parent branch can appear in the merge result
whenNot: Never skip
primaryTradeoff: vs truffleHog: same as Phase 2

name: truffleHog v3.80
label: truffleHog v3.80
changesWhen: Same as Phase 2. Scans the merge commit.
whenToUse: Same as Phase 2 — entropy-based detection for non-patterned secrets in the merge commit
whenNot: Skip when gitleaks is sufficient
primaryTradeoff: vs gitleaks: same as Phase 2

name: GitHub Advanced Security — Secret Scanning
label: GitHub Advanced Security — Secret Scanning
changesWhen: Same as Phase 2. Scans the push to main automatically — no separate CI step needed.
ciIntegration: GitHub native — automatic on push events when GHAS is enabled
whenToUse: Same as Phase 2 — GHAS scans every push to main automatically with no workflow config needed
whenNot: Use gitleaks when GHAS is not available
primaryTradeoff: vs gitleaks: same as Phase 2

name: Docker buildx v0.15 + GHCR
label: Docker buildx v0.15 + GHCR
changesWhen: Registry migration; multi-platform targets change; layer cache policy changes
output: Image pushed to registry with digest
whenToUse: Default for GitHub-hosted runners — single action builds and pushes with layer caching
whenNot: Use kaniko or Buildah when Docker daemon is unavailable
primaryTradeoff: vs kaniko: Docker buildx needs daemon; kaniko is daemonless and runs inside Kubernetes pods

name: kaniko v1.23
label: kaniko v1.23
changesWhen: Kubernetes executor config changes
output: Image pushed to registry with digest
whenToUse: Kubernetes-native CI runners where Docker daemon is unavailable
whenNot: Skip when Docker buildx is sufficient
primaryTradeoff: vs Docker buildx: kaniko runs without daemon; Docker buildx needs daemon access

name: Buildah v1.36
label: Buildah v1.36
changesWhen: Buildah policy.json changes; push credentials change
whenToUse: RHEL or OpenShift environments where Red Hat tooling is standardized
whenNot: Skip when Docker buildx or kaniko is already configured
primaryTradeoff: vs kaniko: Buildah is OCI-native and RHEL-standard; kaniko is designed specifically for Kubernetes CI pods

name: semantic-release v24
label: semantic-release v24
changesWhen: Commit message convention changes; release channel config changes
whenToUse: Repos using conventional commits — fully automated semver tagging and release notes on every merge
whenNot: Use Release Please when a review PR before tagging is required
primaryTradeoff: vs Release Please: semantic-release tags and publishes immediately on merge; Release Please creates a PR for human review first

name: Release Please v4
label: Release Please v4
changesWhen: Release config changes; changelog sections changed
output: Release PR + Git tag on merge of that PR
whenToUse: When the team wants to review and edit release notes before the tag is cut
whenNot: Skip when semantic-release's immediate tagging is acceptable
primaryTradeoff: vs semantic-release: Release Please creates a review PR; semantic-release tags automatically without review

name: Trivy v0.58 (image mode — registry pull)
label: Trivy v0.58 (image mode — registry pull)
changesWhen: CVE database auto-updates; severity threshold changes; new CVEs may appear after push
ciIntegration: aquasecurity/trivy-action scanning the pushed registry digest
whenToUse: Scans the pushed registry image — the actual artifact that will be signed. Never sign before scanning. Invariant I-P3-1.
whenNot: Never skip
primaryTradeoff: vs Phase 2 container scan: Phase 2 scans a local tarball; Phase 3 scans the registry image to catch base image updates that occurred after Phase 2 ran

name: Grype v0.82
label: Grype v0.82
changesWhen: Grype DB auto-updates; Syft SBOM schema changes
whenToUse: When Syft SBOM was generated in the same pipeline run and re-scanning from SBOM is faster than a full registry pull
whenNot: Skip when Trivy is already configured
primaryTradeoff: vs Trivy: Grype re-scans from SBOM (faster); Trivy scans the registry image directly every time

name: Docker Scout
label: Docker Scout
changesWhen: Docker Scout policy rules updated
output: SARIF / JSON + base image upgrade recommendation
whenToUse: Docker Hub users wanting base image upgrade paths alongside CVE findings
whenNot: Skip when Trivy or Grype is already configured
primaryTradeoff: vs Trivy: Docker Scout recommends specific base image upgrades; Trivy reports CVEs without upgrade paths

name: cosign v2.4 (keyless OIDC)
label: cosign v2.4 (keyless OIDC)
changesWhen: OIDC issuer changes; Rekor transparency log endpoint changes
output: Sigstore signature stored in registry alongside image digest
whenToUse: Always — Kyverno admission policy blocks unsigned images before pod start
whenNot: Never skip
primaryTradeoff: vs notation (CNCF Notary): cosign uses Sigstore transparency log for public auditability; notation uses X.509 certificate chains

name: Syft v1.6 + cosign v2.4 attest
label: Syft v1.6 + cosign v2.4 attest
changesWhen: SBOM format migration; attestation format updates; Rekor endpoint changes
output: CycloneDX JSON SBOM attached as cosign attestation to the registry digest
whenToUse: Always — SBOM attestation must be present before Registry stage. Invariant I-P3-2: SBOM after sign.
whenNot: Never skip
primaryTradeoff: vs SPDX format: CycloneDX is NTIA-recommended and VEX-compatible; SPDX is better for license-focused analysis

name: cdxgen v10.1 + cosign v2.4 attest
label: cdxgen v10.1 + cosign v2.4 attest
changesWhen: Java framework changes; cdxgen language plugin updates
output: CycloneDX JSON SBOM (class-level) attested via cosign
whenToUse: Java/Maven/Gradle repos where Syft misses class-level transitive JVM dependencies
whenNot: Use Syft when multi-ecosystem scanning is needed
primaryTradeoff: vs Syft: cdxgen resolves JVM class-level dependencies; Syft is faster and covers more ecosystems

name: slsa-github-generator v2.1
label: slsa-github-generator v2.1
appliesTo: Container images + binaries — GitHub Actions CI
changesWhen: Generator major version update (breaking changes); Rekor endpoint changes
output: SLSA Level 3 provenance JSON attested via cosign
whenToUse: GitHub Actions CI — every build producing a promoted artifact. Invariant I-P3-4: SLSA after sign.
whenNot: Use Tekton Chains when CI runs on Tekton Pipelines, not GitHub Actions
primaryTradeoff: vs Tekton Chains: slsa-github-generator works natively in GitHub Actions; Tekton Chains runs as a Kubernetes controller for Tekton CI

name: Tekton Chains v0.22
label: Tekton Chains v0.22
changesWhen: Tekton Chains config changes; signing backend changes (Sigstore vs KMS)
output: SLSA provenance stored as TaskRun annotation in the cluster
ciIntegration: Installed as Kubernetes controller alongside Tekton Pipelines
whenToUse: Kubernetes-native CI using Tekton Pipelines — Chains runs as a controller and signs every TaskRun automatically
whenNot: Skip when using GitHub Actions — use slsa-github-generator instead
primaryTradeoff: vs slsa-github-generator: Tekton Chains signs every TaskRun without workflow changes; slsa-github-generator requires a specific GitHub Actions workflow structure

name: Language test runner (Jest / pytest / JUnit / go test) + Codecov
label: Language test runner (Jest / pytest / JUnit / go test) + Codecov
tool: Language test runner (Jest / pytest / JUnit / go test) + Codecov
changesWhen: Test framework version change; coverage threshold tightened
output: JUnit XML + Codecov JSON report + PR comment
whenToUse: Every repo with application code — coverage gates block PRs below the threshold
whenNot: Never skip for application repos
primaryTradeoff: vs Coveralls: Codecov has better GitHub PR comment integration and trend graphs; Coveralls is simpler but has fewer features

name: OWASP ZAP v2.15
label: OWASP ZAP v2.15
changesWhen: ZAP ruleset updates; target URL or auth config changes
ciIntegration: zaproxy/action-full-scan against the locally running container
whenToUse: Web applications with exposed HTTP endpoints. Invariant I-P3-6: DAST after tests.
whenNot: Skip for non-HTTP services: batch jobs, CLIs, queue processors, pure gRPC services
primaryTradeoff: vs Nuclei: ZAP does active spider-and-attack scanning; Nuclei runs template-based checks (faster, fewer false-positives)

name: Nuclei v3.3
label: Nuclei v3.3
changesWhen: Template library updated (nuclei-templates repo); target URL changes
whenToUse: API-heavy services where ZAP's spider-based approach produces excessive false-positives
whenNot: Skip when OWASP ZAP full-scan coverage is sufficient for the web app surface
primaryTradeoff: vs OWASP ZAP: Nuclei uses community templates (fast, low false-positive); ZAP does active spider-and-attack (thorough, more false-positives)

name: k6 v0.53
label: k6 v0.53
changesWhen: Load profile changes; SLA thresholds tightened; new endpoints added
whenToUse: Any HTTP service with a defined latency SLA — catches regressions before promotion
whenNot: Skip for internal-only services with no latency SLA
primaryTradeoff: vs Artillery: k6 uses JavaScript scripts; Artillery uses YAML scenario definitions — pick based on team preference

name: Artillery v2.0
label: Artillery v2.0
changesWhen: Load profile changes; WebSocket scenarios updated; target service changes
whenToUse: Node.js teams preferring YAML-based scenario definitions, or services needing WebSocket load testing
whenNot: Skip when k6 is already configured
primaryTradeoff: vs k6: Artillery uses YAML scenarios (easier to read); k6 uses JavaScript (more flexible for complex logic)

name: Gatling v3.11
label: Gatling v3.11
changesWhen: Simulation class changes; load injection profile changes
whenToUse: JVM-heavy teams (Java/Kotlin/Scala) where Gatling's Scala DSL is the team standard
whenNot: Skip when k6 is already configured and team is not JVM-based
primaryTradeoff: vs k6: Gatling uses Scala/Java DSL and has richer HTML reports; k6 uses JavaScript and is simpler to set up

name: slackapi/slack-github-action v2
label: slackapi/slack-github-action v2
changesWhen: Slack channel changes; bot token rotation
whenToUse: Any team using Slack — notifies build success, failure, and image digest on every main build
whenNot: Replace with PagerDuty when build failures require on-call escalation
primaryTradeoff: vs PagerDuty: Slack sends to a shared channel; PagerDuty pages the on-call engineer directly

name: PagerDuty Events API v2
label: PagerDuty Events API v2
changesWhen: PagerDuty service routing key changes; escalation policy changes
whenToUse: When Phase 3 build failures require immediate on-call escalation outside business hours
whenNot: Use Slack for notifications that do not require immediate human response
primaryTradeoff: vs Slack: PagerDuty escalates to the on-call engineer; Slack sends to a channel that may not be monitored off-hours

name: Microsoft Teams incoming webhook
label: Microsoft Teams incoming webhook
changesWhen: Teams channel webhook URL rotates; Teams tenant migration
ciIntegration: aliencube/microsoft-teams-deploy-card or curl to webhook URL
whenToUse: Organizations where Microsoft Teams is the primary communication tool
whenNot: Skip when Slack is already configured
primaryTradeoff: vs Slack: Teams webhook is free with M365; Slack requires a separate Slack workspace account

name: GHCR — GitHub Container Registry
label: GHCR — GitHub Container Registry
changesWhen: Org transfer; retention policy changes
whenToUse: Default for GitHub repos — zero extra cost, tight OIDC integration
whenNot: Use ECR when AWS VPC endpoint access is required
primaryTradeoff: vs ECR: GHCR needs no AWS account; ECR has deeper AWS IAM and lifecycle policy control

name: ECR — Elastic Container Registry
label: ECR — Elastic Container Registry
changesWhen: AWS account migration; lifecycle policy changes
whenToUse: AWS workloads requiring VPC endpoint registry access or cross-account pull policies
whenNot: Skip when not on AWS
primaryTradeoff: vs GHCR: ECR has deeper AWS IAM integration; GHCR needs no AWS account

name: Google Artifact Registry
label: Google Artifact Registry
license: Google Cloud — pay per GB stored + transferred
changesWhen: GCP project migration; service account or Workload Identity changes; repository format changes
whenToUse: GCP workloads requiring Artifact Analysis (CVE scanning), VPC Service Controls, or Binary Authorization integration
whenNot: Skip when not on GCP
primaryTradeoff: vs GHCR: Artifact Registry integrates with GCP Binary Authorization and Artifact Analysis natively; GHCR needs no GCP account

name: Harbor v2.11
label: Harbor v2.11
appliesTo: All container workloads — on-premises or private cloud
changesWhen: Harbor admin policies change; replication rules update; built-in Trivy integration config changes
output: Immutable image digest + built-in scan results
ciIntegration: Docker login + push to Harbor domain
whenToUse: On-premises or air-gapped environments where cloud registries are prohibited by security policy
whenNot: Use GHCR or ECR when cloud-hosted registry is acceptable
primaryTradeoff: vs GHCR: Harbor is fully self-hosted with built-in Trivy scanning and replication; GHCR needs no infrastructure management

name: Docker Hub
label: Docker Hub
license: Commercial — free tier for public images; paid for private
changesWhen: Docker Hub org policies change; rate limit policy changes
output: Image digest in public or private registry
whenToUse: Public open-source images that need maximum pull accessibility across all cloud providers and tools
whenNot: Use GHCR for private repos — Docker Hub's free tier rate-limits unauthenticated pulls
primaryTradeoff: vs GHCR: Docker Hub has maximum public reach; GHCR is free for private GitHub repos with no rate limits

name: cosign v2.4 verify
label: cosign v2.4 verify
changesWhen: OIDC issuer changes; expected workflow ref changes
output: CLI pass/fail — exits non-zero if signature is invalid or missing
whenToUse: Re-verify after push to registry — confirms the pushed digest matches the signed digest before any promotion
whenNot: Never skip — Kyverno also blocks unsigned images at admission but only when a pod is submitted
primaryTradeoff: vs Kyverno admission check alone: cosign verify is an explicit pre-promotion check; Kyverno blocks at deployment but does not block registry storage

name: cosign v2.4 attach sbom
label: cosign v2.4 attach sbom
changesWhen: SBOM format migration (CycloneDX → SPDX)
output: SBOM stored as OCI artifact attached to the registry digest
whenToUse: Always — SBOM must be retrievable from the registry digest before any consumer or promotion can use it
whenNot: Never skip
primaryTradeoff: vs oras CLI: cosign stores SBOM as a Sigstore attestation with provenance; oras stores as an arbitrary OCI artifact without signing

name: slsa-verifier v2.5 + cosign v2.4
label: slsa-verifier v2.5 + cosign v2.4
changesWhen: Generator version updates; Rekor endpoint changes
output: SLSA provenance attached as OCI artifact + verification pass/fail
whenToUse: Always — SLSA provenance must be attached and verified before any promotion starts. SLSA Level 3 requires this.
whenNot: Never skip
primaryTradeoff: vs in-toto verification: slsa-verifier is SLSA-native with no custom layout; in-toto requires a custom supply chain layout file

name: GitHub Actions + yq v4 + peter-evans/create-pull-request
label: GitHub Actions + yq v4 + peter-evans/create-pull-request
changesWhen: GitOps repo structure changes; image digest field path changes in overlays
output: Pull request bumping the image digest in the target environment overlay
whenToUse: Every promotion — the PR is the explicit promotion gate requiring human approval before merge
whenNot: Never use direct push to the GitOps repo — that bypasses the review gate
primaryTradeoff: vs Flux image automation: manual PR gives an explicit review gate; Flux auto-PR removes the human approval step for fully automatic promotion

name: ArgoCD v2.12
label: ArgoCD v2.12
changesWhen: ArgoCD Application config changes; sync policy changes; RBAC policy changes
output: Kubernetes resources reconciled — sync status: Synced / OutOfSync / Degraded
whenToUse: Default GitOps controller — sync Kubernetes manifests from Git with UI visibility and multi-cluster support
whenNot: Use Flux v2 when a UI-free, purely Kubernetes-native controller is required
primaryTradeoff: vs Flux v2: ArgoCD has a UI dashboard and richer RBAC model; Flux is purely API-driven with no UI dependency

name: Flux v2.4
label: Flux v2.4
changesWhen: Flux source / Kustomization CRD changes; image policy changes
output: Kubernetes resources reconciled via Flux CRDs
whenToUse: When Kubernetes-native, UI-free GitOps is required — Flux runs as Kubernetes controllers only
whenNot: Skip when ArgoCD is already deployed
primaryTradeoff: vs ArgoCD: Flux has no UI and is fully CRD-driven; ArgoCD has a richer UI and centralized multi-cluster view

name: cosign v2.4 verify
label: cosign v2.4 verify
changesWhen: OIDC issuer changes; expected workflow ref changes
output: CLI pass/fail — blocks promotion if signature is invalid or missing
whenToUse: Every promotion in every environment — re-verify before deploying to that environment
whenNot: Never skip — this is the only explicit check between Registry and cluster admission
primaryTradeoff: vs relying on Kyverno alone: cosign verify fails the pipeline before deployment; Kyverno only blocks after a pod is submitted to the API server

name: pytest / Newman (Postman CLI) / k6 — per environment type
label: pytest / Newman (Postman CLI) / k6 — per environment type
tool: pytest / Newman (Postman CLI) / k6 — per environment type
changesWhen: Test suite changes; environment-specific endpoint or auth changes
whenToUse: DEV: integration tests. TEST: integration + contract tests. STAGING: regression tests.
whenNot: Replace with Argo Rollouts for PROD — live traffic canary analysis replaces pre-deployment test suites
primaryTradeoff: vs smoke tests only: full env tests catch integration failures; smoke tests only verify the app started and responds

name: GitHub Environments with protection rules
label: GitHub Environments with protection rules
changesWhen: Required reviewers change; wait timer changes; deployment branch policy changes
output: N/A — workflow pauses until explicitly approved
whenToUse: DEV · TEST · STAGING — explicit human approval required before each promotion
whenNot: Replace with Argo Rollouts + Monitor for PROD
primaryTradeoff: vs automatic promotion on test pass: Gate requires explicit approval; auto-promotion promotes on green tests without human sign-off

name: Argo Rollouts v1.8
label: Argo Rollouts v1.8
stage: Argo Rollouts — PROD only (replaces Env tests)
changesWhen: Canary step percentages change; traffic split policy changes
output: Rollout status: progressing / paused / degraded / healthy
ciIntegration: kubectl argo rollouts plugin + Argo Rollouts controller
whenToUse: PROD only — shifts real traffic gradually to the new version instead of big-bang deploy
whenNot: Skip for DEV / TEST / STAGING — use Gate. Use Flagger when Flux is the GitOps controller.
primaryTradeoff: vs Flagger: Argo Rollouts is native to ArgoCD; Flagger is native to Flux — match to the GitOps controller in use

name: Argo Rollouts AnalysisTemplate + Prometheus v3.0
label: Argo Rollouts AnalysisTemplate + Prometheus v3.0
changesWhen: SLO thresholds change; Prometheus query changes; metric names change after instrumentation updates
output: Rollout auto-complete or auto-rollback based on live metric analysis
ciIntegration: Argo Rollouts AnalysisTemplate CRD — no separate CI action needed
whenToUse: PROD only — auto-completes or auto-rolls back based on error rate, latency, and custom SLO metrics
whenNot: Skip for DEV / TEST / STAGING — Gate handles those environments
primaryTradeoff: vs manual monitoring during deploy: automated analysis removes human watch-and-decide; manual monitoring requires someone watching dashboards during the canary window
